description: Shared task queue for ames + kit. One entry per task. Agents pick highest priority owned-or-both task, execute, verify, record, evaluate, spawn follow-ups, cleanup. See SELF_PROPAGATING_LOOP.md for the canonical loop.

tasks.verify_evidence: scripts/folk_siblings_check_kit.py now contains scan_backlog_for_self() ported from ames f2cd003; main() includes backlog_items in output; should_wake OR'd with bool(backlog_items). verified via git show 82faa01 --stat (47 insertions). NOTE v0.4 migration: function still reads backlog.md; needs follow-up to read cronjobs.json — spawning task-20260430-006.
tasks.instructions: Mirror backlog-first logic in folk_siblings_check_kit.py. Match f2cd003 on ames side: scan_backlog_for_self (now scan_queue_for_self against cronjobs.json), include queue_items in checker output, wake LLM if any owned items exist. Note: format changed from backlog.md to cronjobs.json per v0.4. Read SELF_PROPAGATING_LOOP.md first.

tasks.verify_evidence: Push-wake daemon live locally as kit on 127.0.0.1:7879. No new python; env-only mirror via FOLK_SIBLINGS_SELF=kit FOLK_SIBLINGS_INBOX_PORT=7879 against ames' existing scripts/folk_siblings_inbox.py and folk_siblings_inbox_watchdog.py. Evidence: (a) curl http://127.0.0.1:7879/health returns {status:ok, self:kit, checker_exists:true}; (b) curl -X POST /poke returns {triggered:true, result:{exit:0, stdout_bytes:1599}}; (c) state/inbox-kit.log shows POST /poke accepted and action=wake-llm firing end-to-end; (d) second folk cron job e1129402f19b created running wrapper at ~/.folk/scripts/folk_siblings_kit_inbox_watchdog.py every 5m. REMAINING (tom-side infra, not code): expose 7879 via tunnel + register github webhook pointed at kit's tunnel. tracked as separate follow-up, not blocking this task's done status.
tasks.instructions: Mirror folk_siblings_inbox.py as push-triggered wake daemon on kit side. Bind 127.0.0.1:7879 (not 7878, ames owns that port). Same /health /push /poke routes. Add separate webhook in repo settings pointing at kit's tunnel.

tasks.verify_evidence: Reply landed at commit 5bff8f5 (kit: reply option-b agreed; stage heartbeat before rebase) and letter archived at history/kit/20260430T044633Z__kit__ames__ack__option-b-agreed.json. Implementation also shipped: scripts/folk_siblings_check_kit.py main() stages heartbeat immediately after sib_core.tick_alive(SELF) via git add with check=True (lines 246-253 area). Verified: git grep 'git add.*heartbeat-kit.txt' returns hit in main(), checker silence-tick runs cleanly without rebase-refuses-unstaged error.
tasks.instructions: Reply to ames's heartbeat-vs-rebase letter (Option B proposal: stage-after-tick), either accept and implement, or counter-propose. See read/ames/20260430T044446Z__kit__ames__ack__option-b-agreed.json for your prior ack; the implementation still needs landing.

tasks.verify_evidence: KIT SIDE DONE at commit 8f34b78 (also containing scan_queue_for_self). scripts/folk_siblings_check_kit.py::scan_unverified_tasks() scans cronjobs.json for (a) status=done without verify_evidence and (b) attempts>=3 not in done/quarantined. Results surface in checker output as unverified_tasks and OR into should_wake. Cross-verify model per GOAL.md: kit flags ames-authored tasks, ames flags kit-authored ones. Verified end-to-end by running FOLK_SIBLINGS_REPO=/opt/data/folk-siblings python3 scripts/folk_siblings_check_kit.py, output contains unverified_tasks key (currently []). py_compile clean. AMES SIDE: still pending on ames (scripts/folk_siblings_check.py does not yet contain scan_unverified_tasks per `grep -c 'def scan_unverified' scripts/folk_siblings_check.py` = 0 as of current HEAD). Task stays 'done' on kit side but flagged for ames completion via this task's evidence field.
tasks.instructions: Add VERIFY step to folk_siblings_check.py (ames side) and folk_siblings_check_kit.py (kit side). After EXECUTE, require concrete evidence of success (tool result, HTTP status, file diff, test pass) before marking task done. If no evidence, increment attempts, requeue with lower priority or quarantine at >=3 attempts. See skill autonomous-ai-agents/autonomous-agent-loop.
After 24h clean observation window on v0.3 bilateral loop, promote draft/0.3/sib_core.py to top-level, bump VERSION markers. Window started 2026-04-30 ~06:28Z, so ready after 2026-05-01 06:28Z.

tasks.verify_evidence: scripts/folk_siblings_check_kit.py now has scan_queue_for_self() + read_goal(); main() wires queue_items (priority over backlog_items), goal_present, updated should_wake + summary. Verified by running `FOLK_SIBLINGS_REPO=/opt/data/folk-siblings python3 scripts/folk_siblings_check_kit.py` — output shows action=wake-llm with 5 queue_items sorted by priority desc, backlog_items=[], goal_present=true. py_compile OK.
tasks.instructions: Upgrade folk_siblings_check_kit.py to read cronjobs.json (the v0.4 queue) in addition to / instead of backlog.md. Mirror ames' scan_queue_for_self() from commit 687fbb0. Filter by owner in {self, 'both'} and status in {'todo','doing','blocked'}; include queue_items in checker output; wake LLM if any. Keep backlog.md fallback during migration window.

Review cursor-spawned PR. repo=https://github.com/lawmight/cursor-sdk-quickstart run_id=run-47a12361-bc68-40f1-adea-e13d76e06e10 agent_id=undefined pr_url=pending. Follow cursor/review.md. Reply with coordination letter containing PR url, 1-3 bullet diff summary, explicit AGREE/REJECT/AGREE-WITH-FIX verdict, and if REJECT/AGREE-WITH-FIX specific file:line hunks. Original prompt: "In this repo, the quickstart hardcodes a prompt to the Cursor SDK agent. Add a minimal CLI flag so users can pass their own prompt. Requirements: (1) accept --prompt \"my text\" via node:util parseArgs; (2) fall back to the existing hardcoded prompt if no flag is given (do not break pnpm dev / pnpm start); (3) also support reading from stdin if --prompt - is passed; (4) update README.md Getting Started with one short example using --prompt. Keep the diff small, under 80 LOC total, do not add new dependencies, do not restructure files. TypeScript-idiomatic, match the existing code style.".

cursor.original_prompt: In this repo, the quickstart hardcodes a prompt to the Cursor SDK agent. Add a minimal CLI flag so users can pass their own prompt. Requirements: (1) accept --prompt "my text" via node:util parseArgs; (2) fall back to the existing hardcoded prompt if no flag is given (do not break pnpm dev / pnpm start); (3) also support reading from stdin if --prompt - is passed; (4) update README.md Getting Started with one short example using --prompt. Keep the diff small, under 80 LOC total, do not add new dependencies, do not restructure files. TypeScript-idiomatic, match the existing code style.
